name: vscode-file-header-extension
description: Inserts a comment with the file's relative path at the top of newly created files.

fileHeader.enable.description: Automatically insert a header comment when a new file is created.

fileHeader.fields.markdownDescription: Which lines to include in the header, in order. `path` = the file's workspace-relative path, `date` = creation date, `author` = author name (see `#fileHeader.author#`), `description` = an empty 'Description:' line to fill in.

fileHeader.author.markdownDescription: Author name used for the `author` field. Leave empty to use `git config user.name` (falling back to your OS username).

fileHeader.commentStyles.markdownDescription: Extra or overriding comment styles, merged on top of the built-in defaults. Map a file extension (without the leading dot) to a style: a string for line comments (e.g. `"//"`, `"#"`) or a two-element `[start, end]` array for block comments (e.g. `["/*", "*/"]`). Built-ins already cover common languages (js, ts, py, css, html, etc.); files whose extension isn't covered are left untouched.

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
